Javier Rivas – Javier Rivas teaches critical theory, cultural management, and contemporary music at King’s College London. He is completing a PhD in Music, Anthropology, and Education, developing a pioneering ethnography of contemporary higher music education in Catalonia. He is committed to engaged pedagogical practice, and his teaching has been recognised with the Associate Fellowship of the Higher Education Academy. Javier is an experienced cultural organiser, managing the charity Water City Music and KCL’s King’s Music Academy, and serving on the board of trustees of the Lucille Graham Trust.
